user_message: How should I coach Marcus Williams on Alpine Ski House?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-03 17:26 EST
Subject: Follow-up: forecast prep Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with forecast prep. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
Date: 2024-02-23 15:20 EST
Subject: Re: Re: forecast prep Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about forecast prep. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-09 14:23 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Alpine Ski House forecast prep

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about forecast prep. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: forecast prep Discussion - Alpine Ski House
Date: 2024-03-21
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on forecast prep from our last session.

[00:07:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on partner engagement and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:09:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about security compliance. Can we address those?

[00:12:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:15:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:19:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Alpine Ski House: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Stakeholder Map Generation template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct forecast prep decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
